remarks.text: Save the Address, Save the Cloud (KubeVirt VM Migration Story)
remarks.content: Kubernetes is built for containers, and it’s been doing that since it used to run docker as an engine for its containers. But what if you want to add VMs to the mix? After all,... The post Save the Address, Save the Cloud (KubeVirt VM Migration Story) appeared first on Tigera – Creator of Cali
remarks.contentTranslation: Kubernetes是为容器构建的，自从它曾经将docker作为容器的引擎运行以来，它一直在这样做。但是，如果您想将虚拟机添加到组合中，该怎么办？毕竟，...帖子Save the Address, Save the Cloud （ KubeVirt虚拟机迁移故事）首先出现在Tigera – Cali的创建者

remarks.text: Tiered Network Policy: Scaling Kubernetes Security
remarks.content: As Kubernetes clusters scale from a few development sandboxes to massive, multi-tenant production environments, platform teams often find themselves facing a configuration management crisis. A small number of microservices suddenly demand hundreds of individual Kubernetes... The post Tiered Network
remarks.contentTranslation: 随着Kubernetes集群从几个开发沙箱扩展到大规模的多租户生产环境，平台团队经常发现自己面临配置管理危机。少数微服务突然需要数百个单独的Kubernetes... POST分层网络

remarks.name: Aditya Agarwal
remarks.role: General Partner SouthPkCommons, Co-Founder Bevel_Health
remarks.text:
Everything in AI moves so fast.

We are going to get here:
- Run all the agents in the cloud
- Choose any model (frontier, OSS, Chinese, American)
- Choose any harness
- Have full tracing
- Have recursive improvement loops

I know it will be there but can it happen already?
remarks.translation: AI中的一切都发展得如此之快。我们将到达这里： -运行云中的所有Agent-选择任何模型（前沿， OSS ，中文，美国） -选择任何线束-具有完整的跟踪-具有递归改进循环，我知道它会在那里，但它已经发生了吗？
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Sam Altman
remarks.role: AI is cool i guess
remarks.text:
so far at least, i'm pretty sure AI has been net job-creating.

this was not what i expected--although i was much less pessimistic than others, i thought by this level of capability we'd have seen some impact.

it is possible this direction keeps going!
remarks.translation: 至少到目前为止，我很确定AI一直在创造净就业机会。这不是我所期望的--尽管我比其他人悲观得多，但我认为通过这种能力水平，我们会看到一些影响。这个方向可能会继续下去！
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
This is a great post if youre thinking about applied AI in the enterprise. The headline of this post is about what companies have huge upside from AI, but the deepest nuggets are about what AI transformation looks like in an organization.

It’s fundamentally about changing the underlying workflow or business process. As we move from chat tools to agents, those agents actually have to be deployed against workflows, which usually span multiple functions in an org. This is a different way of deploying AI than solely rolling it out to end users. It takes much more work upfront, but the results are the things that actually drive significant ROI. 

“Software asks the employee to adopt a tool, but infrastructure changes the operating layer underneath the employee. The employee should still know what happened, and the process owner should still be able to pause the workflow, change a rule, approve an exception, or pull a person back in when needed. But the value should not depend on someone remembering to use the AI every day.”

The winners of this will be the platforms that can be deployed for specific workflows and business processes with a deep domain expertise. The playbook will often heavily require FDE support, change management, getting data well organized, be able to have comprehensive evals for the workflows, and much more to get right.
remarks.translation:
如果您正在考虑在企业中应用AI，这是一个很好的帖子。这篇文章的标题是关于公司从AI中获得的巨大好处，但最深层次的是关于AI转型在组织中的样子。从根本上说，这是关于改变底层工作流或业务流程。当我们从聊天工具转向座席时，这些座席实际上必须针对工作流进行部署，这些工作流通常跨越组织中的多个职能部门。
这是一种部署AI的不同方式，而不仅仅是将其推广到最终用户。它需要预先做更多的工作，但结果是实际上可以带来显著的投资回报。“软件要求员工采用工具，但基础设施改变了员工下面的操作层。
员工仍应知道发生了什么，流程所有者仍应能够暂停工作流、更改规则、批准例外情况或在需要时将人员拉回。但价值不应取决于某人记得每天使用AI。“获胜者将是可以部署到具有深厚领域专业知识的特定工作流和业务流程的平台。
该剧本通常需要FDE支持、变革管理、妥善组织数据、能够对工作流进行全面评估等等。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Thibault Sottiaux
remarks.role: Codex & ChatGPT OpenAI
remarks.text:
If you were using Codex before, two things to do

1. Keep using Codex. It is for you
2. Tell your friends and family about ChatGPT Work who you always wanted to show Codex but they’re not as technical and don’t work with code every day

and tell me how it goes
remarks.translation: 如果您之前使用Codex ，有两件事要做1.继续使用Codex。适合您2.告诉您的朋友和家人关于ChatGPT Work的事情，你一直想向他们展示Codex ，但他们并不是那么技术性，也不是每天都在使用代码，请告诉我情况如何
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.text: The new GPT-5.6 family: Luna, Terra, Sol
remarks.content: OpenAI's latest flagship model hit general availability this morning , and comes in three sizes: Luna, Terra, and Sol (from smallest to largest). The new models are priced per 1M input/output tokens as Luna $1/$6, Terra $2.50/$15, Sol $5/$30.
remarks.contentTranslation: OpenAI的最新旗舰机型今天早上正式上市，有三种尺寸： Luna、Terra和Sol （从最小到最大）。新型号每100万个输入/输出代币的价格为LUNA $ 1/$ 6 ， TERRA $ 2.50/$ 15 ， SOL $ 5/$ 30。
remarks.translation: 全新GPT-5.6系列： Luna、Terra、Sol
